authority.note: This file proposes horizons only. It moves no code and authorizes no runtime, loader, SQL, DB, Docker, data, or evidence change.

principles_respected: The current app stops growing horizontally.
Inventory and Control Gestion are independent domains.
KPIONE2 is a Control Gestion adapter.
Docker is a reproducible laboratory, not a backup.
Git/GitHub is the knowledge bus between agents.
No future module must depend mandatorily on Streamlit.
No second repository is proposed yet.

b0_temporal_contract_principle.statement: Control Gestion is reproducible ONLY if the following are declared explicitly. No engine may auto-use 'latest raw' and 'latest route'.
b0_temporal_contract_principle.evidence: C002 B0 confirmed the daily surplus (13,470 extra keys over 2026-04-27..2026-05-10) came from reconstructing from complete latest raw snapshots without the productive affected-date window (>= 2026-05-11).

horizons.name: Parity Closure and Temporal Contract Foundation
horizons.objective: Close G0 functional parity and codify the temporal contracts that make Control Gestion reproducible. Nothing downstream is safe until this is done.
horizons.entry_state: B0 root cause CONFIRMED but G0 OPEN: daily rows=21347 with 1 missing key and 56 value differences; weekly times out at 900s.

required_outcomes.title: Close the missing key
required_outcomes.detail: Resolve the single missing KPIONE2 daily key on 2026-05-15 (fecha_visita+cod_rt+cliente_norm). Determine whether it is a precedence, normalization, or window-boundary omission.

required_outcomes.title: Resolve the 56 value differences
required_outcomes.detail: Resolve the 56 remaining value_differences concentrated in week 2026-06-01, which are a route snapshot/value problem, not a row-count problem (down from 1227 after the route16 dedup experiment E3).
required_outcomes.evidence: C002_B0 experiments E3 value_differences=56; H3/H9 PARTIAL

required_outcomes.title: Define route snapshot policy
required_outcomes.detail: Define the authoritative route_snapshot policy for week 2026-06-01: baseline route attributes match ruta_batch 16/17, while the local reconstruction used ruta_batch 18. The policy must state which route snapshot was effective for the productive materialization, not simply the latest week snapshot.
required_outcomes.evidence: C002_B0 H9 PARTIAL; daily route attributes match ruta_batch 16/17 better than 18

required_outcomes.title: Build weekly from staged daily
required_outcomes.detail: Rebuild the weekly fact from a staged daily/route surface instead of recomputing event_scope from raw. The productive-equivalent path must not re-enter the raw/WindowAgg/Sort/HashJoin pipeline before the week filter, which is the cause of the 900s timeout.
required_outcomes.evidence: C002_B0 weekly.dominant_plan_nodes; E4 timeout; LOADER-FACT-004 (refresh stages daily then weekly)

required_outcomes.title: Decide when Docker/local_pg can be destroyed
required_outcomes.detail: Define the destruction criterion for the local_pg container and Docker lab. Proposed: local_pg may be destroyed only once (a) G0 parity is closed AND reproducible from a versioned recipe in git, (b) all six temporal contracts are declared as data/config under version control, and (c) the 9C7A3/9C7A4 evidence manifest hashes remain the rebuildable source of truth, so the container can be regenerated on demand rather than preserved as a backup (principle 5).

horizons.exit_gate: G0 CLOSED: daily exact parity (0 missing, 0 extra, 0 value diffs on the declared window) AND weekly parity reproduced from staged daily.
horizons.docker_role: Primary: local_pg (stock_zero_cg_parity_pg) is the reproducible parity lab. Not a backup.

horizons.name: Temporal Contract Formalization and DB Catalog
horizons.objective: Promote the six temporal declarations from diagnostic findings to explicit, versioned inputs of the CG reconstruction, and build the authoritative DB object/dependency catalog.
horizons.entry_state: G0 closed; contracts proven but not yet codified as first-class inputs.
required_outcomes: Declare affected_date_window, raw_batch_set, route_snapshot_id/version, source_precedence_version, build_version, and daily_fact_staged as explicit reconstruction inputs (design only).
Authoritative DB object + view-dependency catalog with column-level payload_json and row_hash consumers (supersedes the partial ~28-object estimate; C002 Gate G1).
Map raw into raw_necesario | raw_reconstruible | raw_historico using v_cg_latest_batch_by_source plus the declared window as the boundary (C002 Gate G2).
horizons.exit_gate: Temporal contracts are versioned config and the DB catalog is authoritative.
horizons.docker_role: Still the reproducibility lab; used to validate that declared contracts reproduce the closed parity.

horizons.name: Domain Seam: Inventory and Control Gestion
horizons.objective: Establish the seam between the two independent domains inside the existing repo (no new repo, no big-bang). Make capability boundaries explicit and contract-driven.
horizons.entry_state: Contracts and catalog authoritative; app/db.py still concentrates both domains.
required_outcomes: Separate Inventory (fact_stock_venta, ruta_rutero, stock/cliente views) from Control Gestion (cg_raw/cg_core/cg_mart) at the capability-interface level.
Define read-only capability interfaces behind the temporal contracts so consumers depend on contracts, not on db.py internals.
Confirm the static-only db.py functions are runtime-dead via the C002 Program C runtime smoke before any decomposition.
horizons.exit_gate: Both domains are independently describable and testable without one importing the other's internals.
horizons.docker_role: Validates domain seams against the closed parity without touching Supabase.

horizons.name: Adapter Formalization (KPIONE2 and siblings)
horizons.objective: Formalize KPIONE2, KPIONE, and POWER_APP as pluggable Control Gestion source adapters behind the source_precedence contract, decoupling the CG core from any single source format.
horizons.entry_state: Domain seam exists; sources are hard-wired in the v17 loader and v2 views.
required_outcomes: Define an adapter contract: each source maps its raw payload (including payload-only fields like KPIONE2 SP Item ID) to the CG canonical evidence shape.
Make source_precedence_version an explicit adapter-resolution input, not implicit ordering.
Keep payload_json as the canonical surface (it cannot be removed; C001 R08 REJECTED).
horizons.exit_gate: A new CG source could be added as an adapter without editing the CG core reconstruction.

horizons.name: Headless Capability Layer (Streamlit-optional)
horizons.objective: Expose the validated capabilities through a presentation-agnostic interface so no future module mandatorily depends on Streamlit. Streamlit becomes one consumer among possible others.
horizons.entry_state: Domains and adapters formalized; Streamlit is still the only consumer.
required_outcomes: Define a headless capability API surface (read paths) that the current Streamlit screens could call without changing behavior.
Identify which capabilities are generic (reusable) vs Retail-Trust-specific.
Preserve STOCK_ZERO legacy app as a stable consumer of the headless layer (principle 1 and 2).
horizons.exit_gate: At least one capability is demonstrably callable without importing Streamlit.
horizons.docker_role: Lab for headless capability reproducibility; local_pg likely destroyable by end of H4 if H0.5 criterion is met.

horizons.name: Productization Decision Point (no new repo yet)
horizons.objective: Present Bastian with a decision pack on whether and how to productize the generic temporal-contract capabilities, explicitly deferring any second-repository or big-bang decision.
horizons.entry_state: Headless capability layer exists; generic vs specific split documented.
required_outcomes: A decision pack: which generic capabilities (temporal_window_resolution, raw_batch_selection, route_snapshot_versioning, source_precedence, parity_and_reconstruction) could become a product.
Explicit statement that no second repo and no big-bang rewrite is proposed; options are incremental.
Retail-Trust-specific surfaces remain in STOCK_ZERO legacy.
horizons.exit_gate: Bastian has an explicit, evidence-backed choice; nothing is implemented by this horizon.
horizons.docker_role: Optional; only if a productization experiment is approved.

non_authority_notice: This horizon proposal is research synthesis. It authorizes no implementation. H0 is owned by Codex and gated by Bastian decisions on route policy and Docker destruction.
